steps.title: Evaluate all choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — $5x^{3}y+10xy^{2}$: Coefficients 5 and 10 cannot come from distributing $3xy$; the outer factor would need to be something other than $3xy$. ✗
  • Choice B — $6x^{2}y+7y$: $3xy \cdot 2x^2 = 6x^3y$, not $6x^2y$ (one power of $x$ is lost); $3xy \cdot 7y = 21xy^2$, not $7y$. ✗
  • Choice C — $6x^{2}y+21xy$: First term has $x^2$ instead of $x^3$; second term $21xy$ is missing one factor of $y$. ✗
  • Choice D — $6x^{3}y+21xy^{2}$: Matches exactly: $6x^3y + 21xy^2$. That is the correct answer. ✓
